Treatment options 
Treatment regimens for gonorrhea can be found in STI Treatment Guidelines, 2021. 
Use of EPT in the Treatment of Gonorrhea 
Because of EPT’s effectiveness in reducing gonorrhea reinfection rates, 1 CDC has recommended its use since 2006 for the heterosexual partners of patients diagnosed with gonorrhea if it was unlikely the partners would seek timely evaluation and treatment. 2 
At present, the only CDC-recommended treatment of uncomplicated urogenital, anorectal, and pharyngeal gonorrhea is monotherapy with a single intramuscular dose of ceftriaxone 500 mg. If the recommended injectable treatment is not possible, then EPT via a single dose of 800mg cefixime should be considered. 3 CDC continues to recommend EPT for heterosexual men and women with gonorrhea for whom health department partner-management strategies are impractical or unavailable and whose providers are concerned about partners’ access to prompt clinical evaluation and treatment. This document is intended to provide guidance to providers who choose to use EPT for gonorrhea, and to answer frequently asked questions.
